title: Process of obtaining value added products from de-oiled rice bran
abstract: The invention relates to a process of obtaining value-added products from de-oiled rice bran. In particular, the present invention relates to a process of preparing protein, alcohols and silicic acid from de-oiled rice bran. The process for preparing proteins, silicic acids and alcohols from de-oiled rice bran comprises subjecting the de-oiled rice bran to acid hydrolysis to obtain a first reaction product; separating a first solid mass and a first filtrate from the first reaction product; processing the first solid mass to obtain proteins; neutralizing the first filtrate to obtain a second reaction product; separating a second solid mass and a second filtrate from the second reaction product; processing the second solid mass to obtain silicic acids; and processing the second filtrate to obtain alcohols.
claims:
Claims WE C.EATM: 1. A process for preparing proteins, silicic acids and alcohols from de-oiled rice bran, said process comprising the steps of: a. subjecting the de-oiled rice bran to acid hydrolysis to obtain a first reaction product; b. separating a first solid mass and a first filtrate from the first reaction product; c. processing the first solid mass to obtain proteins; d. neutralizing the first filtrate to obtain a second reaction product; e. separating a second solid mass and a second filtrate from the second reaction product; f. processing the second solid mass to obtain silicic acids; and g. processing the second filtrate to obtain alcohols. 2. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein subjecting the de-oiled rice bran to acid hydrolysis comprises treating the de-oiled rice bran with an acid, the acid being inorganic acid selected from a group comprising of hydrochloric acid, sulphuric acid, nitric acid, phosphoric acid, bromic acid, sulfurous acid, thiosulfuric acid, dithionic acid, pyrosulfuric acid, selenic acid, selenious acid, phosphorous acid, hypophosphorous acid, pyrophosphoric acid, polyphosphoric acid, hypophosphoric acid, boric acid, perchloric acid, hypochlorous acid, hydriodic acid, and mixtures thereof. 3. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein subjecting the de-oiled rice bran to acid hydrolysis comprises treating the de-oiled rice bran with hydrochloric acid or nitric acid. 4. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein subjecting the de-oiled rice bran to acid hydrolysis comprises adding hydrochloric acid or nitric acid to de-oiled rice bran to attain pH value in the range of 1.0 to 4.0 and maintaining the same for a time period in the range of 1 to 6 hours at elevated temperature. 5. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein processing the first solid mass to obtain proteins comprises: 

 â¢ subjecting the first solid mass to alkali hydrolysis to obtain a third reaction product; â¢ separating a third solid mass and a third filtrate from the third reaction product; â¢ acidifying the third filtrate to obtain a fourth reaction product; â¢ separating a fourth solid mass and a fourth filtrate from the fourth reaction product; and â¢ obtaining proteins from the fourth solid mass. 6. The process as claimed in claim 5, wherein subjecting the first solid mass to alkali hydrolysis to obtain the third reaction product comprises treating the first solid mass with an aqueous solution comprising an alkali at pH of 8.0 to 12.0 at elevated temperature, the alkali being selected from a group comprising of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, and mixtures thereof. 7. The process as claimed in claim 5, wherein acidifying the third filtrate to obtain the fourth reaction product comprises treating the third filtrate with an acid selected from a group comprising hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, and a mixture thereof at pH of 3.0 to 5.5 at elevated temperature. 8. The process as claimed in claim 5, wherein obtaining proteins from the fourth solid mass comprises: a. dissolving the fourth solid mass in a solvent to obtain a solution; b. drying the solution thus obtained to obtain proteins. 9. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein neutralizing the first filtrate to obtain the second reaction product comprises treating the first filtrate at elevated temperature with at least one of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, and mixtures thereof. 10. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein processing the second solid mass to obtain silicic acid comprises drying the second solid mass to obtain silicic acid. 11. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein the second filtrate comprises Fructo Oligo Saccharides (FOS). 12. The process as claimed in claim 1, wherein processing the second filtrate to obtain alcohols comprises: â¢ subjecting the second filtrate to a fermentation process to obtain a fifth filtrate; and â¢ separating alcohols and proteins from the fifth filtrate. 13. The process as claimed in claim 12, wherein separating the alcohols from the fifth comprises subjecting the fifth filtrate to a rectification column. 14. The process as claimed in any of the aforesaid claims, wherein the fourth filtrate is mixed with the second filtrate and a mixture thereof is processed to obtain alcohols. 15. The process as claimed in claim 12, wherein the proteins thus separated from the fifth filtrate is mixed with the fourth solid mass.
